reasoning: The market probability is 6.5%. A re-acceleration of CPI inflation to 4.1% or above in any month of 2025 requires a substantial overshoot of the current baseline trajectory (around 2.5-3.0%). The most likely catalysts for such an increase would be a major energy shock (e.g., severe Middle East escalation) or the rapid implementation of substantial tariffs (e.g., a hypothetical universal tariff following the 2024 election). While these tail risks exist, inflation is currently cooling, and base effects make a return to >4% difficult without a severe new shock. I estimate the probability of such an event at roughly 7%, very close to the market consensus of 1.5 standard deviations above baseline expectations.
